facts.text: Blodgett Mills earned its name when Loren Blodgett built the first grist and saw mills on the Tioughnioga River in 1806, turning a forested area into a bustling settlement.
The town’s oldest church building, originally erected in 1811 for the Homer Baptist congregation, was moved to Blodgett Mills in 1838 and still stands in use today.
In 1854, the Syracuse, Binghamton & New York Railroad reached Blodgett Mills, making the first train a landmark event that linked the hamlet to regional markets.
Blodgett Mills sits on the eastern edge of the Tioughnioga River, whose waters flow south into the Chenango and ultimately the Susquehanna River.
The community began in the early 1800s as part of the Military Tract, land granted to Revolutionary War veterans and opened for settlement after 1790.
A historic grange chapter, the oldest in Cortland County, was chartered in Blodgett Mills in 1876 to support local farmers through education and mutual aid.
